title: Electric power steering (EPS) structure description - System diagram

blocks.text: Electric power steering (EPS) structure description - System diagram

The steering force of the steering wheel is detected by a torque sensor and the signal is sent to the control unit, which calculates the assist torque using the torque signal and the vehicle speed signal from the PGM-FI ECU and drives the motor to assist the steering force. The engine speed signal is used for fault detection. The EPS control unit sends the motor load status to the PGM-FI ECU. If the electrical load on the motor increases during idling or at low speeds, the PGM-FI ECU increases the idling rpm to avoid engine shutdown. If steering operation is performed during idling stop, the EPS control unit sends an engine restart request to the PGM-FI ECU to start the engine. In addition, an idling stop inhibit signal is output to the PGM-FI ECU to prevent shifting to idling stop during steering. When the EPS system detects an abnormality, it stores the DTC and sends a signal to the combination meter to turn on the EPS warning light.
